name: Río Turón Dams Trail
name_original: Dique del Molino Caído – circular desde El Burgo por el Río Turón
description: Hike the easy 2.9-mile Dique del Molino Caído circular route from El Burgo, following the scenic Turón River through Sierra de las Nieves Na
region_intro: Hiking around El Burgo offers diverse natural landscapes within the Sierra de las Nieves National Park, a protected area recognized for its ecological value. The region features varied topography, including craggy rocks, dense pine forests, and significant riverine ecosystems formed by the Río Turón and its tributaries. Karstic scenery is present in areas like the Sierra Blanquilla, contributing to the unique geological formations. Best hiking trails around El Burgo The most popular hiking route is Río Turón Dams Trail, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 14 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the scenic Río Turón, passing by several dams. Another top favourite among local hikers is Molino Caído Dam – Path Along the Turón River loop from El Burgo, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This loop explores the riverbanks and surrounding natural areas. Local hikers also love the El Burgo – Path through a small river loop from El Burgo, a 9.0 miles (14.5 km) trail leading through river valleys and local landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Hiking in El Burgo is defined by the diverse terrain of the Sierra de las Nieves National Park, featuring pine forests, river valleys, and karstic formations. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging routes. The routes in El Burgo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 2,500 hikers have used komoot to explore El Burgo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in El Burgo?
A: El Burgo offers a diverse range of hiking experiences with over 10 routes available. These trails cater to various fitness levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging mountain ascents within the Sierra de las Nieves National Park.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in El Burgo?
A: Yes, El Burgo has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995690599/rio-turon-dams-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Turón Dams Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.7 km route that follows the scenic Río Turón, offering a pleasant walk for all ages. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17227885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo – El Burgo Village Center loop from El Burgo&lt;/a&gt;, which is around 6.5 km.

Q: Can I find trails with swimming spots in El Burgo?
A: Absolutely. The Río Turón and its tributaries are central to many hiking experiences in El Burgo, and several dams along these rivers create beautiful swimming spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995690599/rio-turon-dams-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Turón Dams Trail&lt;/a&gt; is particularly appealing for those looking to cool down during warmer months, as it passes by several such locations.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in El Burgo?
A: Yes, El Burgo features several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10297907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino Caído Dam – Path Along the Turón River loop from El Burgo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.7 km path that explores the riverbanks and surrounding natural areas. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31525725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo – Path through a small river loop from El Burgo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 14.5 km trail leading through river valleys.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in El Burgo?
A: El Burgo is nestled within the Sierra de las Nieves National Park, a World Biosphere Reserve. You can expect to encounter a varied topography including craggy rocks, dense pine forests (home to Spanish fir trees), and diverse riverine ecosystems along the Río Turón. The grey chalky slopes of the Sierra Blanquilla also offer impressive karstic scenery, adding to the unique geological formations.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or natural attractions along the trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural attractions and viewpoints. You can find stunning panoramic views from locations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wind Pass&lt;/a&gt; (Puerto del Viento), which offers vistas of both the Sierra de las Nieves and the Sierra de Grazalema. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/613806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrecilla Peak (1,919 m)&lt;/a&gt; is another prominent natural monument. For a beautiful water feature, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/719931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Virgen Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in El Burgo?
A: The hiking routes in El Burgo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 2,500 hikers have used komoot to explore El Burgo's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, the beauty of the Río Turón, and the well-maintained paths within the Sierra de las Nieves National Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Burgo?
A: Spring and autumn are generally considered the best times for hiking in El Burgo. The weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, especially if you plan to enjoy the river swimming spots. Winter offers cooler temperatures, but some higher elevation trails might experience colder conditions.

Q: Are there any challenging or difficult hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, El Burgo offers more difficult routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1275431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of Water – Cave of the Three Doors loop from Los Sauces&lt;/a&gt;, which is a demanding 17 km trail with significant elevation changes, taking over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in El Burgo?
A: Many trails in the Sierra de las Nieves National Park, including those around El Burgo, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please check specific trail regulations if you plan to visit sensitive ecological zones.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking paths that pass through El Burgo?
A: Yes, El Burgo is integrated into larger hiking networks. Sections of the Great Málaga Path (GR 249) and the GR243 Long-distance Sierra de las Nieves Path pass through or near the area, offering opportunities for extensive treks and connecting El Burgo to other parts of the region.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Sierra de las Nieves National Park is a World Biosphere Reserve, meaning it's rich in biodiversity. Along the rivers, you might spot various aquatic species. The diverse landscape, from craggy rocks to dense forests, supports a variety of animal life, including mountain goats, eagles, and other native birds. Always observe wildlife from a respectful distance.
tips: Recommended alternative to the signposted hiking trail on the other side of the river.
The Turón river dams are responsible for regulating the flow and controlling excess sediment. From the Molino Caído dam there is a path that goes down to the bathing area, known as Azud Largo.

faqs.question: Is the Dique del Molino Caído trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is considered easy and is very suitable for beginners, families, and those with less hiking experience. Some initial sections, like the 'Manolo 'El Cantaor'' path, are particularly simple and adapted for children.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows the Turón River, offering beautiful riverside views. You'll walk on a mix of natural paths, gravel paths, and some paved sections. The scenery features lush vegetation, small river pools, and views of the surrounding Sierra de las Nieves mountains. The riverbanks are rich with oleanders, willows, poplars, and ash trees.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Dique del Molino Caído route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the trail's namesake, the Molino Caído Dam, as well as other dams like Dique del Nacimiento and Dique de la Hierbabuena. You'll also enjoy the Path Along the Turón River, which features popular bathing spots like Azud Largo.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for swimming along the Turón River?
faqs.answer: Yes, the dams along the river, particularly the Dique del Molino Caído, create attractive bathing areas. The 'Azud Largo' is a popular spot for a refreshing dip, and other river pools can be found along the route, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is highly recommended for its natural beauty and accessibility, making it an enjoyable experience throughout the year. However, for swimming, the warmer months would be ideal.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Dique del Molino Caído trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from El Burgo, and parking is typically available within or near the town, providing access to the trailhead along the Turón River. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for hikers.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, specific regulations regarding dogs within the Sierra de las Nieves Natural Park should be checked. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Sierra de las Nieves National Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to access the Sierra de las Nieves National Park. However, it's always good practice to check for any local regulations or temporary restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: What regulations should I be aware of when hiking in Sierra de las Nieves National Park?
faqs.answer: When hiking in Sierra de las Nieves National Park, it's important to respect the natural environment. This includes staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, not picking plants, and carrying out all your trash. Open fires are strictly prohibited, and camping may require specific permits in designated areas. Always check the park's official guidelines for the most current information.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the El Burgo - Ronda trail, and parts of the Carril del Dique - GR 243. It also briefly touches upon the El Burgo - La Fuensanta and El Burgo - Puerto de la Mujer trails.

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna can be seen along the Turón River?
faqs.answer: The Turón River is known for its rich biodiversity. It's one of the few trout rivers in Málaga province, and its banks are home to a variety of plant life including oleanders, various types of willows, poplars, and ash trees. Keep an eye out for local bird species and other small wildlife that thrive in this riparian environment.
